flags.concern: Step 3 is incomplete: `useDerivedTimeline` cannot be a straight move of the `useMemo` blocks, because `resolvedConfig` currently depends on `configSignatureRef` and `stableResolvedConfigRef` to preserve object identity across poll churn. If that stabilization is dropped or left behind in the orchestrator, the split stops being a pure extraction and risks preview remount/regression.
flags.evidence: `useTimelineData` keeps `configSignatureRef`/`stableResolvedConfigRef` at [useTimelineData.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts#L153) and uses them in the `resolvedConfig` memo at [useTimelineData.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts#L474). [RemotionPreview.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.tsx#L36) explicitly documents that it relies on `useTimelineData` keeping `config.clips` referentially stable.

flags.concern: The plan does not preserve the dependency from save/sync to render state. `renderDirty` is not just render-local UI state; it is flipped to `true` when a save succeeds. Extracting `useRenderState` and `useTimelineSave` without explicitly threading that setter will change behavior.
flags.evidence: `saveTimeline` sets `setRenderDirty(true)` on successful save at [useTimelineData.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts#L217), while `useClientRender` clears it after a render through the setter wired at [useTimelineData.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts#L616). Step 4/6 split these concerns but never specifies how the save hook will still drive `renderDirty`.

flags.concern: Keeping `reloadFromServer` in `useAssetOperations` is the wrong boundary for this repository. That function is a save/sync reset path, not an asset helper, and moving it there forces the asset hook to depend on save internals anyway. This undermines the stated goal of concern isolation.
flags.evidence: `reloadFromServer` at [useTimelineData.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts#L600) loads both timeline and registry, rebuilds full `TimelineData`, resets `editSeqRef`/`savedSeqRef`, calls `commitData`, preserves selection refs, and sets `saveStatus` to `'saved'` at [useTimelineData.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts#L613). The plan assumption says to keep it in `useAssetOperations`, which means the asset hook must reach deep into save/sync state instead of reducing coupling.
